title: The Washington Post - September 25, 1927 (Page 63)
author: Unknown
card_summary: A newspaper clipping from The Washington Post, dated September 25, 1927, page 63. This issue predates Philip K. Dick's birth in December 1928 and may relate to his family background, the Dick or Kindred families, or contextual events in Washington, D.C.
page_summary:
A scanned newspaper page from The Washington Post (Washington, D.C.), dated Sunday, September 25, 1927, page 63. The clipping was sourced from Newspapers.com, originally clipped by researcher pwshelton8296 on April 9, 2025, and downloaded the same day. The PDF text layer contains only source metadata with no recoverable article text.

This is the earliest newspaper clipping in the archive, predating Dick's birth on December 16, 1928 by over a year. The Washington Post was the major newspaper for the nation's capital, where Dick's parents Joseph Edgar Dick and Dorothy Kindred Dick were living at the time. Dick was born in Chicago but his parents had ties to the Washington, D.C. area. This pre-birth clipping may document Dick family activities or the social milieu of his parents before his arrival, making it a potential genealogical or biographical source.
